channel.title: Pratik Dhanave — Blog
channel.description: Long-form writing on multi-agent AI, medical AI governance, HIPAA-aware architecture, and cloud-native systems.

item.title: Five MAF orchestration shapes — adding Group Chat and Magentic
item.description: The first ten posts treated MAF as having four orchestration patterns. The official docs say five. Here are the two I missed — Group Chat and Magentic — with the API surface, when to pick each, and the test path that catches them at build time.

item.title: Lessons from Converting 18 Agents in 90 Days
item.description: What worked, what was hard, and what we'd do differently. Real numbers: 18 agents, 90 days, 5 governance policies, 4 provider swaps.

item.title: Refactor: from rolled-our-own to MAF-native
item.description: I built memory, communication, security, governance, and evals from scratch first. Then I deleted most of it and used the official MAF packages. Here's the audit, the deletions, and the result.

item.title: Deployment & A2A: From ADK's Web Deploy to MAF's Scalable Architecture
item.description: Cloud Run deployments, agent-to-agent communication, load balancing, and production observability.

item.title: Ollama as the default LLM for enterprise-shaped systems
item.description: PROVIDER=ollama, granite4.1:3b, zero API keys, no Azure account. How to make a multi-agent project that demonstrates enterprise patterns run end-to-end on a laptop in 90 seconds.

item.title: Callbacks and Middleware: Porting ADK Hooks to MAF's Decorator Pattern
item.description: Callbacks to middleware: composable decorators for audit, retry, token enforcement, and OpenTelemetry integration.

item.title: Multi-turn evals from first principles
item.description: Single-turn evals check one decision. Multi-turn evals check the whole trajectory. Here's a Python harness — three evaluators (tool order, forbidden tools, LLM judge) — running against local Ollama with mocked tools.

item.title: Provider Abstraction: From Gemini-Only to Swappable LLMs
item.description: Zero-code provider swaps: Ollama (dev), OpenAI (staging), Azure Foundry (prod). Same agents, different models.

item.title: Governance with the Agent Governance Toolkit
item.description: OWASP Agentic Top 10 coverage with YAML policy files, two API surfaces (one-line wrapper and programmatic evaluator), and a metric bridge that shows policy denials in Grafana.

item.title: Tool Wrapping: From ADK Functions to MAF Governed Tools
item.description: From ADK functions to MAF governed tools. Adding policy enforcement, DLP, approval gates, and OPA integration.

item.title: Observability for multi-agent: traces and metrics
item.description: OpenTelemetry through MAF's configure_otel_providers, custom workflow spans, custom metrics for runs / duration / agent invocations / policy decisions, OTel Collector to Prometheus + Jaeger, and a Grafana dashboard you can actually use.

item.title: Token Exchange Patterns: Porting Multi-Turn State from ADK to MAF
item.description: Sessions to threads: porting multi-turn state from ADK to MAF. Token budgeting, long-term memory, and conversation audit trails.

item.title: A2A: when the workflow IS the broker
item.description: The reference architecture distinguishes request-based and message-driven agent communication. For in-process orchestration, MAF's workflow runtime IS the broker. For distributed agent-to-agent, agent-framework-a2a is what you reach for.

item.title: The Executor Pattern: ADK→MAF Conversion for Agentic Control Flow
item.description: How to port ADK's orchestration callbacks to MAF builders without losing control. The executor pattern: you own the loop.

item.title: Memory done right with MAF
item.description: AgentSession is short-term memory. MemoryContextProvider + MemoryFileStore is long-term memory. Mem0 is long-term memory when you want it hosted. Here's the shape — and the one boundary the framework gets right that your custom memory layer probably doesn't.

item.title: Why We Migrated from Google ADK to Microsoft MARA
item.description: The philosophy, trade-offs, and what we learned converting 18+ agents in 3 months. Provider abstraction as the foundation for portable agents.

item.title: Agent registry as a project-local convention
item.description: The Microsoft Agent Framework deliberately does not ship an agent registry. Here's why that's the right call, and what the @register decorator pattern looks like when you build one yourself.

item.title: Four orchestration patterns in MAF — and when to pick each
item.description: Sequential, Concurrent, Handoff, and Custom WorkflowBuilder. Four shapes the Microsoft Agent Framework ships out of the box. Each one is the right answer to a different question.

item.title: The 12-chapter reference architecture, in 102 Python files
item.description: Microsoft published a 12-chapter reference architecture for multi-agent systems and a separate framework (MAF) to build them. I implemented one on top of the other and learned what each chapter actually demands in code.

item.title: OpenTelemetry and Evaluation in Multi-Agent Workflows — the full production stack
item.description: How to instrument multi-agent systems with OpenTelemetry, propagate trace context across an in-memory bus, and build a layered evaluation pipeline — from real-time policy gates to async LLM-as-judge to SLO-based trust scoring. Everything I learned building Genie.
